name: python
description: Python interpreter. Preferred install path is pyenv so versions are user-local and switchable.

installs.notes: After install, pyenv needs shell init lines in the rc file (`eval "$(pyenv init -)"` plus PYENV_ROOT/PATH exports), then `pyenv install 3.12` and `pyenv global 3.12`.
Single global version, no init dance.

Two-step review flow (never pipe curl to bash). pyenv builds Python from source, so build deps are prerequisites: on Debian/Ubuntu `sudo apt-get install -y build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev`.
Installs Python 3.12. Ensure 'Add to PATH' is selected. A new terminal is needed.

commonFailures: pyenv shims don't take effect until the rc-file init lines exist and the shell restarts — `python3` keeps resolving to the system interpreter.
pyenv `install` fails mid-build with cryptic errors when build dependencies (openssl, zlib, readline headers) are missing.
On macOS, `python` (no 3) does not exist by default; scripts expecting it need an alias or pyenv global.
